title: The New Deal
description: Test you knowledge on the New Deal!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0a35d6ec2456edf8f67ff3530035760.jpg)

>-Source: Vaughn Shoemaker, "Alphabet Agencies", [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Editorial_cartoon_mocking_FDR%27s_%22Alphabet_agencies%22.jpg) 1935

**The image most directly illustrates a United States domestic policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: creating programs, agencies, and laws that aimed to ease the conditions of the Great Depression
instituting advanced degree requirements for federal employees and establishing a merit-based system for employment
reorganizing and combining executive departments to reduce federal spending and address the growing deficit
establishing federal agencies intent on investigating national and international crimes

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0a35d6ec2456edf8f67ff3530035760.jpg)

>-Source: Vaughn Shoemaker, "Alphabet Agencies", [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Editorial_cartoon_mocking_FDR%27s_%22Alphabet_agencies%22.jpg) 1935

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The New Deal
The Neutrality Acts
The Great Migration
The Fourteen Points

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0a35d6ec2456edf8f67ff3530035760.jpg)

>-Source: Vaughn Shoemaker, "Alphabet Agencies", [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Editorial_cartoon_mocking_FDR%27s_%22Alphabet_agencies%22.jpg) 1935

**The image most strongly supports which of the following arguments about Roosevelt’s actions during the Great Depression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Roosevelt aimed to solve the economic crisis by creating several programs to offer immediate relief to Americans.
Roosevelt’s programs sought to help white people more than black people suffering from the Great Depression.
Roosevelt’s programs were unsuccessful and did not lead to the end of the Great Depression.
Roosevelt expanded the power of the presidency beyond the limits of his constitutional powers.

assessment_items.question_content:
![Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d56ad1a8622db6d686750ed3fd22e0f6.gif)

>*Description: Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"*

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Theres_nothing_the_matter.gif), 1937

**Which of the following statements is the most accurate interpretation of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court was impeding economic recovery by overturning New Deal programs.
The Supreme Court was refusing to hear cases about the constitutionality of New Deal laws.
The Supreme Court had too many conservative justices who sought to increase the president’s powers.
The Supreme Court had too much power to check the executive branch and was violating the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
![Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d56ad1a8622db6d686750ed3fd22e0f6.gif)

>*Description: Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"*

**Which of the following developments is the most direct cause of the perspective expressed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Supreme Court ruling that New Deal laws were an unconstitutional use of federal power
the president’s plan to pack the court with younger justices after several unpopular rulings
the Supreme Court’s expanded powers after the ratification of the Twenty-first Amendment
the president’s efforts to ratify four new amendments to expand presidential power over the economy

assessment_items.question_content:
![Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d56ad1a8622db6d686750ed3fd22e0f6.gif)

>*Description: Franklin Roosevelt, dressed as a doctor, talks to the man with the words, "U.S. Constitution" written on his chest. In the background, a nurse with "Supreme Court" written on her smock watches their conversation with a disgruntled face. There is also a medical bag that has "New Deal Remedies" printed on the front of it. FDR says, "There's nothing the matter with you  — Your nurse is not giving you proper treatment!"*

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Theres_nothing_the_matter.gif), 1937

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: President Roosevelt attempted to change the composition of the Supreme Court.
President Roosevelt proposed an amendment to limit the Supreme Court’s checking power.
President Roosevelt passed a series of executive orders so that the Supreme Court could not declare the programs unconstitutional.
President Roosevelt stopped enforcing New Deal programs, letting the economy recover naturally.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Some historians have pictured the New Deal as the latest round in . . . the ‘ceaseless conflict between man and the dollar.’ But the distinctive feature of the political revolution which Franklin D. Roosevelt began and Truman inherited lies not in its resemblance to the political wars of Andrew Jackson or Thomas Jefferson, but in its abrupt break with the continuity of the past. If, as Charles A. Beard contended, the Civil War was the ‘Second American Revolution,’ the toppling of the dominance held by the Republicans for nearly three-fourths of a century can be considered as the Third American Revolution.” 

>-Source: Samuel Lubell, journalist, *The Future of American Politics,* 1952

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the 1930s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: realignment of the Democratic Party
establishment of regulatory financial agencies
expansion of the Supreme Court
entrance into World War II

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Some historians have pictured the New Deal as the latest round in . . . the ‘ceaseless conflict between man and the dollar.’ But the distinctive feature of the political revolution which Franklin D. Roosevelt began and Truman inherited lies not in its resemblance to the political wars of Andrew Jackson or Thomas Jefferson, but in its abrupt break with the continuity of the past. If, as Charles A. Beard contended, the Civil War was the ‘Second American Revolution,’ the toppling of the dominance held by the Republicans for nearly three-fourths of a century can be considered as the Third American Revolution.” 

>-Source: Samuel Lubell, journalist, *The Future of American Politics,* 1952

**Which of the following most directly led to the changes described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an economic crisis that led to many hardships, including mass unemployment
an increasing demographic shift of people from urban areas to rural areas
a change in foreign policy towards intervention in foreign countries
a series of scandals within the Republican Party that shocked the American public

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Some historians have pictured the New Deal as the latest round in . . . the ‘ceaseless conflict between man and the dollar.’ But the distinctive feature of the political revolution which Franklin D. Roosevelt began and Truman inherited lies not in its resemblance to the political wars of Andrew Jackson or Thomas Jefferson, but in its abrupt break with the continuity of the past. If, as Charles A. Beard contended, the Civil War was the ‘Second American Revolution,’ the toppling of the dominance held by the Republicans for nearly three-fourths of a century can be considered as the Third American Revolution.” 

>-Source: Samuel Lubell, journalist, *The Future of American Politics,* 1952

**Which of the following political shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: African Americans and religious minorities became the primary base of the Democratic Party
Evangelical Christians and other social conservatives became the Republican Party base
Factions splintered off from the Democratic Party because of conflicting interests
Members of the Republican Party shifted the party towards more conservative policies

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Population in Urban and Rural territory, 1930 to 1960** ||
 | $1930$ | $1940$ | $1950$ | $1960$
- | - | -
Urban | $69,160,599$ |$74,705,338$ | $90,128,194$ | $113,063,593$
Rural | $54,042,025$ | $57,459,231$ | $61,197,604$ | $66,259,582$

>-Source: [US Census](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)


**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1930 to 1950 shown in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: need for war production during World War II, opening urban economic opportunities
decreased immigration from Latin America, primarily Mexico and Panama
creation of affordable housing with long-term mortgages in the suburbs
government programs that provided housing for impoverished families

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Population in Urban and Rural territory, 1930 to 1960** ||
 | $1930$ | $1940$ | $1950$ | $1960$
- | - | -
Urban | $69,160,599$ |$74,705,338$ | $90,128,194$ | $113,063,593$
Rural | $54,042,025$ | $57,459,231$ | $61,197,604$ | $66,259,582$

>-Source: [US Census](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**The major trend depicted in the table most directly reflects which of the following developments in the United States during the mid-twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Americans moved to cities to find jobs.
Immigration from Latin America decreased.
African Americans were the primary labor force.
Improvements to the economy ended the Great Depression.

assessment_items.question_content:
|| **Population in Urban and Rural territory, 1930 to 1960** ||
 | $1930$ | $1940$ | $1950$ | $1960$
- | - | -
Urban | $69,160,599$ |$74,705,338$ | $90,128,194$ | $113,063,593$
Rural | $54,042,025$ | $57,459,231$ | $61,197,604$ | $66,259,582$

>-Source: [US Census](https://www.census.gov/history/pdf/histstats-colonial-1970.pdf)

**Which of the following statements best explains the change over time in population in urban and rural areas depicted in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The population in urban areas increased at a faster rate than the population in rural areas.
The population in urban areas increased as the population in rural areas decreased.
The population in urban areas increased at the same rate as the population in rural areas.
The population in urban areas increased at a slower rate than the population in rural areas.
